query: add a GET /health endpoint that responds 200 with JSON body `{"status":"ok"}`. Do NOT touch the existing /users handler or imports that are already present.
check: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp $FILE $tmp/main.go && (cd $tmp && go build -o server ./main.go) && $tmp/server & pid=$!; sleep 1; body=$(curl -sf -o /dev/stdout -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:8080/health); kill $pid 2>/dev/null; code=${body: -3}; json=${body%???}; [ "$code" = 200 ] && echo "$json" | jq -e '.status=="ok"'

query: replace the final `log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil))` with a graceful shutdown: construct an *http.Server with Addr ":8080", serve in a goroutine, then in main wait on a channel fed by signal.Notify for syscall.SIGINT and syscall.SIGTERM, and call srv.Shutdown with a 5-second context timeout. Do not change the handlers.
check: gofmt -e $FILE >/dev/null && tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp $FILE $tmp/main.go && (cd $tmp && go build -o server ./main.go 2>&1) && grep -q 'syscall.SIGTERM\|os.Interrupt' $FILE && grep -q 'srv.Shutdown\|server.Shutdown' $FILE && grep -q '5\s*\*\s*time.Second' $FILE

query: extract the anonymous function passed to http.HandleFunc("/users", ...) in main() into a top-level function `func usersHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)` with the same body, and register it via `http.HandleFunc("/users", usersHandler)`. Behavior must be preserved.
check: gofmt -e $FILE >/dev/null && tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cp $FILE $tmp/main.go && (cd $tmp && go build -o server ./main.go) && grep -q 'func usersHandler' $FILE && grep -q 'http.HandleFunc("/users", usersHandler)' $FILE
